result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the change:

## Root Cause

In `ParameterListWrappingRule`, the `needToWrapParameterList()` method determines whether a parameter list should be wrapped to multiple lines. For `ktlint_official` code style, when a lambda like `{ size -> icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await() }` appeared on a line exceeding `max_line_length`, the rule would incorrectly wrap the lambda's parameter list, producing the weird indentation:

```kotlin
{
        size ->
    icon?.getBitmap(size)?.await()
}
```

The existing exemption for `ktlint_official` (`isPartOfFunctionLiteralStartingOnSameLineAsClosingParenthesisOfPrecedingReferenceExpression`) was too narrow - it only exempted trailing lambdas of call expressions. Other single-line function literals (like those assigned to variables or passed as arguments) were not exempted.

## Fix

Added a new check `isPartOfFunctionLiteralOnSingleLine()` in `ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` (line 153-158) that returns `true` when the VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST is part of a FUNCTION_LITERAL that doesn't contain any newlines (i.e., is on a single line). This check is evaluated before the `isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength()` check, so single-line lambdas won't have their parameters wrapped just because the overall line is long.

The change is minimal: one new method and one new condition in the `when` block, in a single file (`ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`).
